title: Finite Steffensen Method Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite Steffensen bad accelerated value QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact rational Steffensen replay for the affine half-step row g(x)=(x+1)/2 from x0=0 computes steffensen_value = 1, while a malformed row claims steffensen_value = 3/2, isolating a compact scalar equality conflict for the Farkas route.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional Steffensen or fixed-point acceleration rows only when they add distinct denominator-safety, contraction, convergence-rate, nonlinear fixed-point, floating-point, or theorem-reconstruction pressure; keep general Steffensen method theory and stability claims in future Lean or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed affine map, source iterate, two ordinary fixed-point iterates, first differences, second difference, correction, accelerated value, and residuals as deterministic rational replay obligations.
Route the false accelerated value through checked QF_LRA/Farkas evidence after replay computes the exact value.
Expose the pack through sequence-tail, exact-vs-floating, bounded theorem-shadow, real-analysis, numerical-analysis, and learner query surfaces.

Keep general Steffensen convergence, convergence-order improvement, fixed-point existence, nonzero-denominator preconditions, floating-point implementation correctness, and numerical-stability claims marked as future Lean or numerical-honesty work until formal routes exist.
